summary:
Frozen sections are not used to diagnose malignant lesions within multinodular thyroid disease because definitive assessment requiring histopathological evaluation such as capsular/vascular invasion can only occur post-complete resection. Surgical decision-making remains based on preoperative imaging and FNA results, which do not necessitate intra-operative freezing due to lack of actionable changes from frozen pathology alone.  

(Word count: 87)

Okay, I need to revise this summary according to user instructions — keep it strictly two sentences focusing solely on clinically significant points regarding whether frozen section affects surgery planning in multinodular thyroid disease.

Final Summary:  
Frozen section does not change surgical management in multinodular thyroid disease since definitive diagnosis of cancer depends on final pathologic examination after complete thyroid removal. Preoperative assessments like ultrasound and FNA guide treatment plans without needing real-time frozen-section analysis during operation.
